issues.message: Accepted 51 claims; region target is at most 40.
Skipped 38 source chunks due to configured chunk budgets.

scaffold.case_question: For generally healthy adults, should eggs be treated as meaningfully harmful, neutral, or beneficial in dietary advice, especially with respect to cardiovascular risk?

epistemic_config_profile.description: For policy questions grounded in studies, guidelines, evaluations, and implementation evidence.
epistemic_config_profile.label: Empirical Policy Decision

profile_evidence_sections.description: Evidence about effect size and direction.
profile_evidence_sections.title: Effect Evidence

profile_relation_types.description: One claim increases support for another without being the decisive dependency.
profile_relation_types.use_when: Use for direct evidence or argument support.

profile_relation_types.description: One claim undercuts or contradicts another.
profile_relation_types.use_when: Use for contrary evidence, validity objections, or failed assumptions.

profile_relation_types.description: One claim narrows the population, endpoint, mechanism, condition, or interpretation of another.
profile_relation_types.use_when: Use when the boundary being refined is explicit.

profile_relation_types.description: Claims are close enough that a reviewer may consider merging them.
profile_relation_types.use_when: Use only for near-duplicate or strongly overlapping claims.

profile_relation_types.description: The force of one claim depends on a condition, implementation detail, or prerequisite.
profile_relation_types.use_when: Use for conditional recommendations, feasibility dependencies, and assumptions.

profile_relation_types.description: One claim is a decision crux for another or for the question.
profile_relation_types.use_when: Use when changing belief in one claim would materially change the decision read.

profile_relation_types.description: Claims can both be partly true but pull the decision in different directions.
profile_relation_types.use_when: Use for tradeoffs, external-validity limits, and evidence-vs-implementation tensions.

quality_checks: every required source should contribute at least one useful claim unless genuinely irrelevant
claims must be entailed by exact excerpts
relations must use only accepted claim IDs and ontology relation types
the final map should expose cruxes, scope limits, and source-role boundaries
near-duplicate claims should be merged or given distinct roles
relation_goals: Look for measurement-validity and external-validity claims before treating effect evidence as decisive.
Represent implementation and cost conditions as depends_on or in_tension_with edges.
connect at least one crux/scope-limit claim to a conclusion-support claim
preserve tensions instead of flattening them
use source limitations to bound claim strength
prefer cross-source relations when they clarify disagreement or scope

limitations: Check design, population, endpoint, and confounding.
May combine evidence with policy judgment.
Imported by the case initializer; provenance and completeness have not been independently audited.

target_claim_roles.description: Evidence or reasoning that supports a candidate answer.
target_claim_roles.use_when: A claim bears directly on the likely answer.

target_claim_roles.description: A claim whose truth would materially change the answer.
target_claim_roles.use_when: The decision turns on this uncertainty.

target_claim_roles.description: A boundary on where a claim applies.
target_claim_roles.use_when: Population, context, timing, or evidence limits matter.

target_claim_roles.description: A practical condition for applying a recommendation.
target_claim_roles.use_when: Feasibility, compliance, cost, operations, or rollout matters.

target_claim_roles.description: Context needed to interpret the evidence.
target_claim_roles.use_when: Useful context that is not itself load-bearing.

target_claim_roles.description: A useful claim that does not fit the other roles.
target_claim_roles.use_when: Use sparingly when no sharper role fits.

target_claim_roles.description: A claim about whether measured outcomes represent the construct of interest.
target_claim_roles.use_when: Outcome choice, proxy quality, or measurement error matters.

target_claim_roles.description: A claim about whether evidence transfers across populations, settings, or time.
target_claim_roles.use_when: Generalizing from studies or pilots is uncertain.

target_claim_roles.description: A claim about resource needs, cost, or operational feasibility.
target_claim_roles.use_when: Adoption depends on budget or implementation capacity.
